zh.Langley, Shropshire, England: 英國史洛普爾明日香 安德 盧可利
zh.lifeSummary: Edward Burnel於1320年出生在英國史洛普爾明日香 安德 盧可利。他和Joyce Botetout至少有1個女兒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Edward Burnel于1320年出生在英国史洛普爾明日香 安德 盧可利。他和Joyce Botetout至少有1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: Burnel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Burnell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Burnel, a metathesized form of Old French Brunel, originally a nickname for someone with brown hair or complexion.

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Edward Burnel was born in 1320, in Langley and Ruckley, Shropshire, England. He had at least 1 daughter with Joyce Botetout.

fr.lifeSummary: Edward Burnel est né en 1320, à Langley and Ruckley, Shropshire, Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Joyce Botetout.

de.lifeSummary: Edward Burnel wurde 1320 in Langley and Ruckley, Shropshire, England geboren. Er hatte mit Joyce Botetout mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Edward Burnel è nato il nel 1320 nel Langley and Ruckley, Shropshire, England. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Joyce Botetout.

ja.Langley, Shropshire, England: イギリスシュロプシュアイアラングリー アンド ルックリー
ja.lifeSummary: Edward Burnelは1320年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアラングリー アンド ルックリーで生まれた。彼はJoyce Botetoutとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Burnel(은)는 1320년 영국 슈럽실르 랭글리 앤드 럭리에서 태어났다. 그는 Joyce Botetout(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Edward Burnel nasceu em 1320, em Langley and Ruckley, Shropshire, Inglaterra. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Joyce Botetout.

ru.lifeSummary: Edward Burnel родился в 1320 г. в местности лангли анд руккли, шропшир, Англия. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Joyce Botetout.

es.lifeSummary: Edward Burnel nació en 1320, en Langley and Ruckley, Shropshire, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Joyce Botetout.
